articles.title: GOOGLE UPDATES GMAIL FOR IPHONE WITH MATERIAL 3 REDESIGN
articles.summary: Google is updating Gmail for iPhone with a Material You redesign, introducing a refreshed bottom navigation bar for improved usability. The new design, which features a modern, rounded aesthetic and enhanced visual hierarchy, aligns with Google's latest UI principles. This update aims to create a more cohesive experience across Google's ecosystem while making navigation intuitive for users.

articles.title: 2025 NATIONAL DESIGN AWARD WINNERS
articles.summary: The National Design Awards celebrate innovation and the impact of design. The 2025 winners include Kim Hastreiter as a Design Visionary, with ilumiNACIÓN recognized for Climate Action. Other categories include architecture, communication, digital, fashion, interior, landscape, and product design.

articles.title: IS APPLE REALLY LOSING ITS ADVERTISING TOUCH?
articles.summary: Apple's latest billboard ads have sparked criticism for their uninspired design and lack of the brand's usual creative polish. The campaign features simple text-based messages on a white background. It has been called dull and unmemorable compared to Apple's typically sleek and visually striking marketing. Designers argue that while minimalism can be effective, these ads feel more like placeholders than intentional, compelling brand statements.

articles.title: DESIGNING FOR CLARITY: HOW WE RESTRUCTURED INTERCOM'S INFORMATION ARCHITECTURE
articles.summary: Intercom restructured its Information Architecture to enhance clarity and the user experience by grouping similar features, refining AI integration, reducing noise, and streamlining settings. It simplified navigation, improved organization, and introduced visual consistency through research and iterative design.

articles.title: ERROR MESSAGE USABILITY
articles.summary: Jakob Nielsen's ninth usability heuristic emphasizes clear, actionable error messages that help users recognize, diagnose, and recover from issues. Effective design reduces frustration, saves time, and builds trust. Good error messages use plain language, specify problems, and offer solutions, ensuring users stay informed and engaged rather than confused or frustrated.

articles.title: BRILLIANT OR BAFFLING? DESIGNERS ARE TORN OVER THE NEW KELLOGG'S BILLBOARDS
articles.summary: Kellogg's new ad campaign boldly highlights its logo to emphasize its status as the original breakfast choice. While some praise it as a top-tier print ad, others argue it's more for ad professionals than everyday consumers.

articles.title: 100+ OPEN SOURCE SVG SPINNERS
articles.summary: This collection of animations was curated from various sources. All have an MIT License, so they can be used commercially without restrictions and require no attribution.

articles.title: INTERACTIVE MAPS AND DATA VISUALIZATION
articles.summary: Jsvectormap is a lightweight JavaScript library for creating interactive maps and pretty data visualization. It supports all modern browsers, including IE9+.

articles.title: NEW WAY TO ORGANIZE AND COLLABORATE ON DESIGN ASSETS
articles.summary: This minimalist collaboration platform for creative teams lets you manage tasks, get AI reviews, and share work all in one place.

articles.title: TIMELESS BRANDING AND VISUAL IDENTITY FOR BUDAPEST INTERNATIONAL FILM FESTIVAL
articles.summary: Studio STOKI designed BIFF's branding with a bold, modern look that blends Eastern Bloc aesthetics with contemporary design. Using blue as the hero color, the identity focuses on typography and film stills, with playful illustrations on merchandise to keep it fresh and distinctive.

articles.title: HOW DESIGNERS RE-CREATED ANNE FRANK'S SECRET ANNEX IN STRIKING DETAIL
articles.summary: A replica of Anne Frank's secret annex has opened in New York, offering a detailed, lived-in recreation unlike Amsterdam's empty original. Designed with historical accuracy, the exhibit highlights Anne's life and the Holocaust's impact.

articles.title: HOW TO BE HAPPIER (FOR DESIGNERS)
articles.summary: Designers often struggle with feedback, strategy, and career growth. Shifting to an active role in feedback, understanding organizational strategy, and broadening responsibilities can improve satisfaction. Fulfillment, not happiness, should be the goal. Adapting expectations and exploring leadership roles can lead to meaningful impact and career advancement in design or beyond.

articles.title: TOM BINGHAM'S FANTASTICAL ILLUSTRATIONS ARE A WELCOME REMINDER TO SLOW DOWN AND STAY COSY
articles.summary: Tom Bingham loves creating whimsical characters inspired by nature, old RPGs, and his childhood love for comics.

articles.title: ARTIST SCALES-UP EVERYDAY OBJECTS AND REIMAGINES THEM AS UNIQUE BUILDING DESIGNS
articles.summary: Felipe de Castro transforms everyday objects into architectural designs, reimagining earrings, potato chips, and napkins as creative structures showcased in his artistic drawings.

articles.title: BIOPHILIC DESIGN EXPLAINED: SIX ELEMENTS FOR A NATURE-INSPIRED HOME
articles.summary: Integrating nature into indoor spaces, Biophilic Design enhances well-being through environmental features, natural patterns, light, and local materials.
